2022-08-01.title: Using Nuclear Reactors for Cover, Russians Lob Rockets at Ukrainians
2022-08-01.abstract: Russia has turned Europe’s largest nuclear power plant into a fortress, stymying Ukraine’s forces and unnerving locals who fear both shelling and a radiation leak.

2022-08-01.title: Video Shows Ship Loaded With Grain Leaving Ukraine Port
2022-08-01.abstract: A ship loaded with Ukrainian grain left the Port of Odesa for the first time since Russia’s invasion, following an international deal aimed at lowering global food prices.

2022-08-01.title: A Pelosi Trip to Taiwan Would Test China’s Appetite for Confrontation
2022-08-01.abstract: Xi Jinping has long vowed to answer any challenge to China’s claim to Taiwan. But current economic and political forces may make him unlikely to court a crisis.

2022-08-01.title: England Exults After Its Women’s Soccer Team Wins a Major Trophy
2022-08-01.abstract: The dramatic 2-1 victory over Germany in the European Championship final touched off celebrations that have been more than 50 years in the waiting.

2022-08-01.title: Ukraine wages a ‘deep war’ to degrade Russian forces as Moscow continues its scorched-earth campaign.
2022-08-01.abstract: An attack on a train over the weekend was the latest in a series of strikes that have disrupted Russian logistical efforts and inflicted grave losses.

2022-08-02.title: War Spurs Ukrainian Efforts to Legalize Same-Sex Marriage
2022-08-02.abstract: The role of gay soldiers, the lack of legal rights for their partners, and the threat of Russia imposing anti-L.G.B.T. policies have turned the war into a catalyst for change in Ukraine.

2022-08-02.title: As a grain shipment heads to Lebanon, fighting intensifies on many fronts.
2022-08-02.abstract: A ship loaded with Ukrainian corn was heading for an inspection in Turkish waters on Tuesday, a first under the provisions of a recent grain deal that opened Ukrainian ports for export after five months of a wartime Russian blockade.

2022-08-02.title: Gentle Into That Good Night? Not Boris Johnson.
2022-08-02.abstract: The British prime minister may be leaving Downing Street, but few expect him to cede the spotlight, or abandon the prospect of one day regaining his position.

2022-08-02.title: As Pelosi Arrives, Quiet Defiance in Taiwan
2022-08-02.abstract: Public nonchalance about the tensions over Nancy Pelosi belies a political reality: Many are tired of China’s threats and crave American support.

2022-08-02.title: The Illegal Airstrips Bringing Toxic Mining to Brazil’s Indigenous Land
2022-08-02.abstract: The Times identified hundreds of airstrips that bring criminal mining operations to the most remote corners of the Amazon.

2022-08-03.title: Taiwan’s leader, Tsai Ing-wen, has vowed to defend its sovereignty.
2022-08-03.abstract: Taiwan’s leader, Tsai Ing-wen, owes her presidency to her pledge to preserve the island’s sovereignty. Her meeting with Speaker Nancy Pelosi on Wednesday, and Beijing’s response to the visit, are highlighting the complexity of that task.

2022-08-03.title: Pelosi’s Taiwan Visit Risks Undermining U.S. Efforts With Asian Allies
2022-08-03.abstract: The Biden administration has built an economic and diplomatic strategy in Asia to counter China, assuring friendly countries that the U.S. is in the region for the long haul.

2022-08-03.title: Living side by side, Ukrainian and Russian sailors are tested by the war.
2022-08-03.abstract: Commercial vessels have become some of the few places where Russians and Ukrainians, who make up 15 percent of the world’s 1.9 million sailors, still live side by side on routes around the world while their countries are at war. Some ships have become rare havens of understanding and forgiveness. On other ships, the mood has become tense and at times unbearable, upending the maritime tradition of sailors viewing each other as teammates, no matter their backgrounds.

2022-08-03.title: Chinese military drills circling Taiwan set up a potential standoff.
2022-08-03.abstract: China’s plans to conduct live-fire drills into Taiwan’s territorial seas could mark a new stage of brinkmanship.

2022-08-03.title: Pelosi draws crowds as she moves through Taiwan’s capital.
2022-08-03.abstract: TAIPEI, Taiwan — As Speaker Nancy Pelosi moved around Taiwan’s capital with her entourage on Wednesday, taking one meeting after another, the people of Taiwan shadowed her nearly every step of the way.

2022-08-04.title: Chinese Missiles Strike Seas Off Taiwan, and Some Land Near Japan
2022-08-04.abstract: A day after Nancy Pelosi’s trip, China has begun military drills near Taiwan. They appear to be designed as a trial run for sealing off the island. Five missiles landed in Japanese waters.

2022-08-04.title: Mines, Fires, Rockets: The Ravages of War Bedevil Ukraine’s Farmers
2022-08-04.abstract: A deal allowing ships to start carrying grain from Ukraine solved a logistical problem, but it left a more pressing one for Ukrainian farmers: growing and reaping crops in a war zone.

2022-08-04.title: Brittney Griner’s trial nears an end with closing arguments.
2022-08-04.abstract: The trial of Brittney Griner reached its final stage on Thursday, with the American basketball star appearing in a Russian court for the start of closing arguments in a case that is expected to end with the conviction of Ms. Griner, who has been caught up in the deep crisis between Moscow and Washington over Russia’s invasion of Ukraine.

2022-08-04.title: Long a Climate Straggler, Australia Advances a Major Bill to Cut Emissions
2022-08-04.abstract: Experts said the pledge to reduce emissions by at least 43 percent from 2005 levels by 2030 was both a leap forward and a first step.

2022-08-04.title: Russia’s Shortfalls Create an Opportunity for Ukraine, Western Officials Say
2022-08-04.abstract: Severe equipment and manpower problems could slow President Vladimir V. Putin’s mission as the war enters its sixth month.

2022-08-05.title: China Sends Ships and Planes Toward Taiwan, Defying Rising Criticism
2022-08-05.abstract: Beijing also halted talks with the United States on the military, climate change and other issues, sending ties between the two powers into a deeper chill.

2022-08-05.title: Driven by its first lady, Ukraine raises $5.4 million for off-road ambulances.
2022-08-05.abstract: Olena Zelenska has recently become more visible in advocating for humanitarian causes.

2022-08-05.title: Putin and Erdogan find more room to cooperate at a meeting in Sochi, Russia.
2022-08-05.abstract: The Turkish and Russian leaders sat face-to-face for the second time in less than three weeks, solidifying Turkey’s role as a bridge to the West and a mediator between Russia and Ukraine.

2022-08-05.title: ‘Captain Condom’ Turned the Tide in Thailand’s War on AIDS and Overpopulation
2022-08-05.abstract: At a time when most Thais avoided discussing safe sex and family planning, Mechai Viravaidya promoted condom use with spectacle and humor, saving millions of lives.

2022-08-05.title: Fight or Surrender: Taiwan’s Generational Divide on China’s Threats
2022-08-05.abstract: In areas close to the military drills, younger Taiwanese worried about the future, while older residents looked back to a harsher past to find hope.

2022-08-06.title: Why Australia’s Climate Bill Matters
2022-08-06.abstract: After years of inaction, the world’s third largest exporter of fossil fuels decides to get serious about global warming

2022-08-06.title: Ukrainian Grandmaster Bids to Unseat Russian Head of World Chess Body
2022-08-06.abstract: Hoping to end Russia’s domination of the International Chess Federation, Andrii Baryshpolets is running for the presidency of the powerful governing organization.

2022-08-06.title: Chinese Military Drills Aim to Awe, Both Abroad and at Home
2022-08-06.abstract: As China staged its third day of war exercises, state media was also working hard to shape the narrative, in what seemed a response to earlier public discontent.

2022-08-06.title: An Afrocentric Fashion Boom in the Streets and Presidential Palace
2022-08-06.abstract: The wardrobe of Francia Márquez, Colombia’s first Black vice president, is the creation of a young designer at the center of an Afro-Colombian fashion explosion.

2022-08-06.title: In Turbulent Times, Xi Builds a Security Fortress for China, and Himself
2022-08-06.abstract: Taiwan, Covid, “color revolutions,” the classroom: everywhere, China’s leader sees threats that foreign forces can exploit, and he has enlisted the whole nation to defend against them.

2022-08-07.title: Maps: Tracking Tensions Between China and Taiwan
2022-08-07.abstract: Maps showing where Chinese military aircraft and unmanned drones are flying close to Taiwan.

2022-08-07.title: Fighting around a Ukrainian nuclear plant heightens safety fears.
2022-08-07.abstract: DRUZHKIVKA, Ukraine — Fighting raged on Saturday near a sprawling nuclear power plant in the south of Ukraine, despite warnings from nuclear safety watchdogs earlier this week that conditions there were posing risks and “out of control.”

2022-08-07.title: After China’s Military Spectacle, Options Narrow for Winning Over Taiwan
2022-08-07.abstract: The exercises were designed to deter Taiwan from moving further away from Beijing, but they also indicated how few policy carrots China has.

2022-08-07.title: A U.S. Return to Guadalcanal, in Another Tense Historical Moment
2022-08-07.abstract: At a ceremony to mark the 80th anniversary of a crucial battle in the Pacific, two daughters of men who served there reflected on the lessons of war.

2022-08-07.title: ‘Jewel in the Crown of Corruption’: The Troubles of Kenya’s China-Funded Train
2022-08-07.abstract: Five years after its launch, the railway has become associated with debt, dysfunction and criminal inquiries, and it is now a lightning rod in Tuesday’s election.

2022-08-08.title: In My Homeland, the Smell of Death on a Summer Afternoon
2022-08-08.abstract: In Ukraine, tragedies are a backdrop to everyday existence, piling up in numbers that seem inconceivable, an inescapable reality that feels like the very air in your lungs.

2022-08-08.title: For Ukrainians in Donbas, tragedy is the backdrop to everyday existence.
2022-08-08.abstract: LYSYCHANSK, Ukraine — There was a mass grave that held 300 people, and I was standing at its edge. The chalky body bags were piled up in the pit, exposed. One moment before, I was a different person, someone who never knew how wind smelled after it passed over the dead on a pleasant summer afternoon.

2022-08-08.title: Freed From Prison in China, Taiwan Activist Urges Resistance to Beijing’s Threats
2022-08-08.abstract: Li Ming-che and his wife, who met with Speaker Nancy Pelosi in Taiwan last week, have sought to inspire others not to be intimidated into silence by Beijing.

2022-08-08.title: China’s Military Drills and Other Tensions With Taiwan, Explained
2022-08-08.abstract: China’s military announced new exercises near Taiwan, a day after concluding four days of drills that simulated a blockade of the island.

2022-08-08.title: Rumors spread quickly in wartime Ukraine.
2022-08-08.abstract: While seemingly harmless, these tales may have their origins in Russian propaganda, according to analysts monitoring Russian disinformation campaigns in Ukraine.

2022-08-09.title: Heavy Rain Floods Streets in South Korea, Leaving Several Dead
2022-08-09.abstract: A record-breaking storm hit Seoul and surrounding provinces in South Korea, causing deaths. More rainfall is expected.

2022-08-09.title: Record-Setting Rainfall Inundates Seoul, Killing at Least 9
2022-08-09.abstract: The South Korean capital saw some of its heaviest rainfall in decades. Drivers abandoned cars in the upscale Gangnam district as roads became impassable.

2022-08-09.title: For Ukrainians Abroad, War Has Also Meant a Flowering of Identity
2022-08-09.abstract: Italy already had the biggest Ukrainian community in Western Europe before the war, but in recent months the diaspora has taken on new prominence and visibility.

2022-08-09.title: After being trapped for months, ships loaded with grain have left Ukraine. Where are they going?
2022-08-09.abstract: Two of the ships that have left ports in Ukraine after months of being trapped there are going to Turkey, carrying corn. One is going to England. One to Ireland. Others are headed to Italy and China.

2022-08-09.title: Will the fifth time be the charm for Raila Odinga?
2022-08-09.abstract: Since his first presidential run three decades ago, Mr. Odinga has been at the center of most of Kenya’s elections.

2022-08-10.title: From the Workshop to the War: Creative Use of Drones Lifts Ukraine
2022-08-10.abstract: While Russia lacks an effective long-range strike drone, Ukraine’s experimentation has produced an array of inexpensive, plastic aircraft, jury-rigged to drop grenades or other munitions.

2022-08-10.title: A tally of damage near a Crimean air base contradicts the Kremlin’s assertions.
2022-08-10.abstract: ODESA, Ukraine — Damage from a series of explosions at a Russian air base in Crimea appeared to be greater than the Kremlin has described, as officials on the Russia-occupied peninsula on Wednesday declared a state of emergency and said that dozens of nearby homes and commercial structures had been hit.

2022-08-10.title: What Martial Law? Marcoses Get Star Treatment in New Film.
2022-08-10.abstract: A big-budget production depicts the family as victims of a political vendetta, a popular narrative during the recent presidential election in the Philippines.

2022-08-10.title: ‘We Couldn’t Do Anything’: Family Drowns in Seoul Basement During Floods
2022-08-10.abstract: Hundreds of thousands of poor people live in semi-underground homes around the city. The death of a family of three showed ​how vulnerable they are to flooding.

2022-08-10.title: In the Amazon, a U.N. Agency Has a Green Mission, but Dirty Partners
2022-08-10.abstract: One of the world’s largest sustainable development agencies has worked with energy companies to quash opposition and keep oil flowing, even in sensitive areas.

2022-08-11.title: The Jan. 6 attack was a crisis. So why wasn’t it more of a scandal?
2022-08-11.abstract: The political dog that didn’t bark.

2022-08-11.title: Zelensky addresses the explosions in Crimea as photos suggest a major loss for Russia.
2022-08-11.abstract: With satellite images showing at least eight wrecked warplanes after explosions at a Russian air base in Crimea, President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine said late Wednesday that Moscow’s mounting losses should persuade its leadership to “find a way out of the war.”

2022-08-11.title: North Korea Says Its Covid Outbreak Is Over
2022-08-11.abstract: Kim Jong-un declared “victory” over the virus, despite a lack of vaccines. Outside experts have cast doubt on the North’s pandemic-related claims.

2022-08-11.title: Rishi Sunak Has a Sterling Résumé. It’s Not Helping Him Replace Boris Johnson.
2022-08-11.abstract: Mr. Sunak is viewed by many in his party as too distant from ordinary Britons, and is being blamed by some for setting off the rebellion that toppled Prime Minister Boris Johnson.

2022-08-11.title: As China Vows More Military Exercises, Taiwan Is Undeterred
2022-08-11.abstract: China’s drills have hardened Taiwan’s understated approach to navigating a big-power rivalry over its political future.

2022-08-12.title: Serbia’s Leader Rejects ‘Little Putin’ Label Amid Fears of Russian Meddling
2022-08-12.abstract: President Aleksandar Vucic’s ties to Moscow raise awkward questions as the Kremlin appears to be stirring unrest in the Balkans to deflect attention from the war in Ukraine.

2022-08-12.title: A U.N. ship will carry Ukrainian grain directly to countries worst affected by shortages.
2022-08-12.abstract: It is the first ship hired to carry the grain to countries badly affected by food shortages since a deal between Ukraine and Russia allowed shipments to resume.

2022-08-12.title: A Greek Scandal Reverberates as Eavesdropping Expands in Europe
2022-08-12.abstract: Revelations that the cellphone of a top opposition politician was tapped have shaken the government and stoked concerns over just how widespread such surveillance is.

2022-08-12.title: Why are Russia and Turkey growing so close? Putin needs allies, and Erdogan needs bolstering.
2022-08-12.abstract: BRUSSELS — Turkey’s mercurial president, Recep Tayyip Erdogan, is in political difficulty before elections next year, with his economy imploding, a central bank nearly out of foreign exchange and volcanic inflation running at about 80 percent annually.

2022-08-12.title: Showdown at the Mansion Gates: How Sri Lankans Rose Up to Dethrone a Dynasty
2022-08-12.abstract: An army of nuns, farmers and middle-class professionals felt a duty to save their virtually bankrupt nation. But their fight is far from over.

2022-08-13.title: Aid helps Ukraine achieve ‘outweighed gains.’
2022-08-13.abstract: As the war drags on, Ukraine has managed to hold off Russian gains for the past month thanks in large part to continued support from the United States and its European allies, and help on the ground from partisans.

2022-08-13.title: American Lawyer Is Released From Detention in U.A.E.
2022-08-13.abstract: Asim Ghafoor, who was convicted of financial crimes, was freed from a monthlong detention in Dubai after paying a $1.36 million fine.

2022-08-13.title: Ivory Coast Supplies the World With Cocoa. Now It Wants Some for Itself.
2022-08-13.abstract: A new generation of Ivory Coast chocolatiers is striving to change an industry that has long left cocoa farmers in poverty.

2022-08-13.title: His Next Move: A Ukrainian Boy Starts a New Life Through Chess
2022-08-13.abstract: After fleeing the war in Ukraine with his mother, Maksym Kryshtafor, 8, is using his passion for chess to help him assimilate into the United Kingdom.

2022-08-13.title: Restaurants That Are Institutions as Much as Places to Dine
2022-08-13.abstract: Edmonton’s Bistro Praha is among the rare spots to find a formula that has ensured longevity in a generally difficult business.

2022-08-14.title: Eight Injured in Shooting in Jerusalem
2022-08-14.abstract: Two of the victims were seriously hurt by gunfire outside the Old City. Israeli authorities described the incident as a terrorist attack.

2022-08-14.title: Shots Fired at Airport in Australia’s Capital
2022-08-14.abstract: There were no reports of injuries, but the episode was nonetheless shocking in a country where mass and random shootings are extremely rare.

2022-08-14.title: Japanese Photographer Blows Whistle on Treatment of ‘Comfort Women’
2022-08-14.abstract: While Tsukasa Yajima has won praise for exposing problems at South Korea’s best-known shelter for former sex slaves, he has also been the focus of intense backlash.

2022-08-14.title: The first U.N. ship carrying Ukrainian grain for Africa prepares to depart.
2022-08-14.abstract: The Brave Commander, a freighter, is expected to carry 23,000 metric tons of wheat to Ethiopia, which World Food Program officials said was “on the verge of famine.”

2022-08-14.title: A Boom, a Fire and a Stampede: Dozens Die at a Coptic Church in Egypt
2022-08-14.abstract: A blaze that killed at least 41 at a church in greater Cairo caused anguish among a religious minority that has long felt itself oppressed in Egypt.

2022-08-15.title: The focus of fighting shifts to Ukraine’s south.
2022-08-15.abstract: ZAPORIZHZHIA, Ukraine — The main front in Russia’s military onslaught on Ukraine appears to have shifted dangerously to the south of the country, risking a catastrophe at Europe’s largest nuclear power plant and setting up a possible make-or-break struggle for an important regional capital seized by Russia at the start of its invasion.

2022-08-15.title: Troops, Noodles and Familial Love: China Lays Out Its Ideal Taiwan
2022-08-15.abstract: Beijing has seized on the tensions over Taiwan, further fanned by a meeting between five U.S. lawmakers and the island’s president, to push a far-reaching vision for unification.

2022-08-15.title: Brittney Griner Appeals Conviction Amid Talk of Prisoner Swap
2022-08-15.abstract: The appeal process could take months, but a senior Russian diplomat said that talks with the United States about a possible prisoner exchange were underway.

2022-08-15.title: Myanmar Widens Arrests and Slaps Aung San Suu Kyi With More Prison Time
2022-08-15.abstract: The ousted civilian leader has been convicted on corruption charges by a military-appointed court. A Japanese journalist and two models have also been detained in recent weeks.

2022-08-15.title: Iran Denies Any Involvement in Rushdie Attack
2022-08-15.abstract: But a spokesman for Iran’s Foreign Ministry, in the first remarks from the government since the assault, said the prizewinning author had crossed “red lines.”

2022-08-16.title: Ready to Party. But First Comes the Cleanup.
2022-08-16.abstract: More than 100,000 buildings in Ukraine have been destroyed in the war. Volunteers are clearing away the rubble — and partying while they do it.

2022-08-16.title: Leadership Vacuum Heightens Worries as Crises Loom in U.K.
2022-08-16.abstract: As energy prices and inflation soar under a caretaker prime minister, critics say transition at the top is leaving Britons in limbo at a tumultuous moment.

2022-08-16.title: Clean Toilets, Inspired Teachers: How India’s Capital Is Fixing Its Schools
2022-08-16.abstract: The Aam Aadmi Party, which rose to power in New Delhi, is overhauling an education system that serves as a lifeline for millions of families looking to break the cycle of poverty.

2022-08-16.title: The Secret Powers of an Australian Prime Minister, Now Revealed
2022-08-16.abstract: Scott Morrison was busy during the pandemic. In addition to being prime minister, he covertly put himself in charge of five ministries. Critics say he damaged democracy.

2022-08-16.title: After a Bitter Contest, Kenyan Opposition Leader Rejects Election Result
2022-08-16.abstract: Raila Odinga said the result announced on Monday, declaring his rival William Ruto as president-elect, should be annulled. The dispute looks set to go to court.

2022-08-17.title: Behind Enemy Lines, Ukrainians Tell Russians ‘You Are Never Safe’
2022-08-17.abstract: Clandestine resistance cells are spotting targets, sabotaging rail lines and killing those deemed collaborators as they seek to unnerve Russian forces.

2022-08-17.title: Mexico Sees Its Energy Future in Fossil Fuels, Not Renewables
2022-08-17.abstract: The president’s push to bring the energy sector under state control has put up roadblocks to renewable energy and left Mexico’s climate goals behind.

2022-08-17.title: North Korea Launches Two Missiles in First Test Since June
2022-08-17.abstract: The test came as South Korea was gearing up for joint military drills with the United States and offered economic assistance to the North.

2022-08-17.title: Ukraine’s top commander says fighting at the front is ‘intense but fully controlled.’
2022-08-17.abstract: KYIV, Ukraine — Ukraine’s top commander has called the situation on the front lines “intense but fully controlled,” saying that Russian forces were trying to advance on multiple fronts as Ukrainian forces continued to grind away at their combat capabilities.

2022-08-17.title: Israel and Turkey to Restore Full Diplomatic Ties
2022-08-17.abstract: A four-year chill in relations has thawed in recent months, and the two nations, once close regional allies, will now exchange ambassadors again.

2022-08-18.title: Sleepy Greek Port Becomes U.S. Arms Hub, as Ukraine War Reshapes Region
2022-08-18.abstract: The flow of U.S. military supplies through Alexandroupoli has angered Russia and Turkey. Now, firms with ties to Russia and America are competing for control of the port.

2022-08-18.title: In Bucha, Burials Continue for Victims of Wartime Atrocities
2022-08-18.abstract: More bodies were interred in the Kyiv suburb this week. They had no names, only numbers.

2022-08-18.title: U.K. Train Strikes Bring More Summer Disruptions
2022-08-18.abstract: Travelers resigned themselves to additional days of struggles as workers seeking higher pay walked out, a day after Britain reported its highest inflation rate in decades.

2022-08-18.title: Europe’s Scorching Summer Puts Unexpected Strain on Energy Supply
2022-08-18.abstract: The dry summer has reduced hydropower in Norway, threatened nuclear reactors in France and crimped coal transport in Germany. And that’s on top of Russian gas cuts.

2022-08-18.title: Factory Shutdowns, Showers for Pigs: China’s Heat Wave Strains Economy
2022-08-18.abstract: A severe drought has dented energy supplies and disrupted access to water for hundreds of thousands. Cities order rolling blackouts; farmers rush to save crops.

2022-08-19.title: Odesa Is Defiant. It’s Also Putin’s Ultimate Target.
2022-08-19.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin knows that Ukraine’s fate, its access to the sea and its grain exports hinge on Odesa. Without it, the country shrivels to a landlocked rump state.

2022-08-19.title: U.N. Chief, Visiting Odesa, Lauds Progress on Grain Deal but Urges Rich Nations to Do More
2022-08-19.abstract: António Guterres, the United Nations secretary general, witnessed grain shipments, one of his organization’s most concrete achievements.

2022-08-19.title: Could New Zealand Change Its Name?
2022-08-19.abstract: A petition before Parliament asks that the country be called “Aotearoa,” which loosely translates from Maori as the “land of the long white cloud.”

2022-08-19.title: Drink Up, Japan Tells Young People. I’ll Pass, Many Reply.
2022-08-19.abstract: The country’s tax agency, hoping to reverse the alcohol industry’s pandemic doldrums, is holding a contest to encourage more drinking among the young.

2022-08-19.title: For Former Rebel in West Africa, Her Allegiance Still Lies With Russia
2022-08-19.abstract: The Kremlin armed a generation of freedom fighters in Africa, like Joana Gomes, who helped Guinea-Bissau win liberation. So her decision to take Moscow’s side in its war with Ukraine was never in doubt.

2022-08-20.title: Germany’s Stony-Faced Chancellor Faces Critics on Nearly Every Side
2022-08-20.abstract: After eight months in office, Olaf Scholz is still struggling to find his footing, drawing questions over a tax scandal and criticism of his response to a Holocaust remark made at his side.

2022-08-20.title: In India, New Wave of Trauma as 11 Convicted of Rape and Murder Walk Free
2022-08-20.abstract: Just as Bilkis Bano had started to rebuild her life after grisly communal violence in 2002, a state government cut short her assailants’ life sentences.

2022-08-20.title: Odesa is defiant. It’s also Putin’s ultimate target.
2022-08-20.abstract: ODESA, Ukraine — The Odesa Fine Arts Museum, a colonnaded early-19th-century palace, stands almost empty. Early in Russia’s war on Ukraine, its staff removed more than 12,000 works for safe keeping. One large portrait remained, depicting Catherine the Great, the Russian empress and founder of Odesa, as a just and victorious goddess.

2022-08-20.title: Why inspectors haven’t yet visited the Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant.
2022-08-20.abstract: Russia appears to be relenting somewhat on access to the nuclear complex, but fears remain about the risks of sending inspectors while the fighting persists.

2022-08-20.title: Canada’s Growing Linguistic Diversity
2022-08-20.abstract: A new report from Canada’s census agency shows that one in four people speak a language other than English or French as their native tongue.

2022-08-21.title: Albania says it arrested Russians and a Ukrainian suspected of spying on a weapons factory.
2022-08-21.abstract: The authorities in Albania arrested two Russian men and a Ukrainian woman suspected of espionage on Saturday after one of the men was found inside a weapons factory, Albania’s Defense Ministry said.

2022-08-21.title: The Face of China: A Globetrotting Diplomat Armed With U.S. Admonitions
2022-08-21.abstract: While Xi Jinping hasn’t visited another country in the pandemic, his foreign minister, Wang Yi, has been to dozens, extolling Beijing’s vision for the world.

2022-08-21.title: ‘Artistic Awakening’ in Benin as Return of Royal Artifacts Attracts Huge Crowds
2022-08-21.abstract: As more looted art comes back to Africa, countries have wrestled with the right way to display it. That 200,000 people have lined up for a show suggests Benin has found an answer.

2022-08-21.title: Aleksandr Dugin, a chief promoter of Russia’s war in Ukraine, is sometimes called ‘Putin’s philosopher.’
2022-08-21.abstract: The Russian writer, whose daughter died in a car blast on Saturday outside Moscow, has espoused a vision of a Russian “world empire.”

2022-08-21.title: Shabab Attack on Somali Hotel Ends With 21 Dead, Officials Say
2022-08-21.abstract: The assault was the most sustained and complex in the capital, Mogadishu, since a new president was elected in May. Officials said more than 100 were wounded.

2022-08-22.title: What We Know About Imran Khan, Pakistan’s Former Prime Minister
2022-08-22.abstract: Mr. Khan’s party has had a comeback since he was ousted this year. He’s now facing charges of violating a terrorism law after he threatened to sue government officials.

2022-08-22.title: The Question Menacing Brazil’s Elections: Coup or No Coup?
2022-08-22.abstract: President Bolsonaro has warned of voter fraud and suggested he would dispute a loss in October’s vote, but the political establishment believes he lacks support to stage a coup.

2022-08-22.title: People are paying to have personal messages painted on Ukrainian artillery shells.
2022-08-22.abstract: The messages have become an inventive, if macabre, way to raise money for resistance to Russia’s invasion.

2022-08-22.title: A Russian spy agency blames Ukraine in the bombing death of Daria Dugina.
2022-08-22.abstract: Kyiv has denied involvement in the death of Ms. Dugina, the daughter of a nationalist ideologue who backed Russia’s invasion of Ukraine.

2022-08-22.title: There will be no talks with Russia if it holds show trials, Zelensky says.
2022-08-22.abstract: It was not the first time Mr. Zelensky has tried to draw a red line on talks as the war approaches the six-month mark.

2022-08-23.title: Pakistan’s Imran Khan Is Now the Target of Forces He Once Wielded
2022-08-23.abstract: Old allies like the military have turned against him, but the former prime minister’s appeal on the street has only grown stronger, setting up a dangerous showdown.

2022-08-23.title: In Vancouver, Indigenous Communities Get Prime Land, and Power
2022-08-23.abstract: After acquiring some of the biggest and most coveted parcels of land in Vancouver, the city’s three First Nations are becoming players in the biggest game in town — real estate.

2022-08-23.title: In Ukraine, a Nuclear Plant Held Hostage
2022-08-23.abstract: Five months after Russian forces took over the Zaporizhzhia plant, all that stands between the world and nuclear disaster are dedicated Ukrainian operators working at gunpoint.

2022-08-23.title: Ukrainians flood a Red Cross hotline for help finding missing loved ones.
2022-08-23.abstract: Workers in Switzerland answer hundreds of calls a day, many from relatives who are overwhelmed with distress.

2022-08-23.title: In China’s Version of ‘Minions’ Movie, Morality Triumphs
2022-08-23.abstract: An apparent censor-added epilogue specially for Chinese moviegoers changed the tone of the comedy and earned online mockery from viewers.

2022-08-24.title: Corrections: Aug. 24, 2022
2022-08-24.abstract: Corrections that appeared in print on Wednesday, Aug. 24, 2022.

2022-08-24.title: 6 Months Into War, Ukraine and Russia Are Both Reshaped
2022-08-24.abstract: It has been six months since Russian forces swept into Ukraine. This is what the conflict looks like for the combatants, and to a worried continent trying to maintain solidarity.

2022-08-24.title: Thousands of Civilian Deaths and 6.6 Million Refugees: Calculating the Costs of War
2022-08-24.abstract: Six months after Russia invaded Ukraine, the human and financial tolls are incalculable. But the figures that have emerged paint a bleak picture.

2022-08-24.title: For civilians near the front lines, Independence Day is just another day of war.
2022-08-24.abstract: Ukrainian authorities restricted mass gatherings to mark the holiday and have urged people across the country to be on guard.

2022-08-24.title: On the eve of Ukraine’s Independence Day, Zelensky vows to take back Crimea.
2022-08-24.abstract: He spoke with the same resoluteness and composure as he has almost every day for the last six months, the same furrow between his brows, the same conviction in his voice.

2022-08-25.title: How China Could Choke Taiwan
2022-08-25.abstract: China is honing its ability to blockade Taiwan, giving Beijing the option of cutting off the self-ruled island in its campaign to take control of it.

2022-08-25.title: ​Decades After a ‘Living Hell,’ Korean Victims​ Win a Step Toward Redress
2022-08-25.abstract: Brothers Home was supposed to help get people off the streets, but a government commission has confirmed its role in detentions and other abuses was a “grave human rights violation by the state.”

2022-08-25.title: Pakistan Court Gives Imran Khan Another Reprieve From Arrest
2022-08-25.abstract: The police were ordered not to arrest the former prime minister, who has been charged under Pakistan’s antiterrorism act, before a hearing set for next Wednesday.

2022-08-25.title: As France Swelters, Private Jets Come Under Attack
2022-08-25.abstract: Politicians are proposing regulating or banning flights by such planes after a summer of extreme heat and soaring energy prices prompted growing calls to tackle the causes of climate change.

2022-08-25.title: The Front-Runner to Replace Boris Johnson Wants to Be a New Iron Lady
2022-08-25.abstract: Campaigning to be Britain’s next prime minister, Liz Truss has modeled herself after Margaret Thatcher and pushed a party orthodoxy of lower taxes, less government and bucking the European Union.

2022-08-26.title: The Long Tail of Covid-19 Disinformation
2022-08-26.abstract: Almost all of Australia and New Zealand’s pandemic restrictions have been lifted. Yet the protests continue.

2022-08-26.title: ‌A Social Media Star of a Changed Middle East: An Arab From Israel in Dubai
2022-08-26.abstract: Nuseir Yassin, a Palestinian citizen of Israel with nearly 60 million followers, has taken advantage of new diplomatic ties to move to the United Arab Emirates — angering many Palestinians.

2022-08-26.title: With Military Attacks and Mockery, Ukraine Pokes the Russian Bear
2022-08-26.abstract: Driven by deep anger at Russia and the need to rally support at home and abroad, Ukraine is regularly goading its much more powerful antagonist.

2022-08-26.title: Zelensky is under pressure over when and how to launch a long-anticipated counteroffensive.
2022-08-26.abstract: Both sides are preparing for a protracted war, but Ukraine has an incentive to counterattack this fall, before the rainy season.

2022-08-26.title: Fighting Between U.S. and Iran-Backed Militias Escalates in Syria
2022-08-26.abstract: Two strikes on U.S. military sites have been tied to forces aligned with Iran, raising tensions just as Washington appears close to striking a new nuclear deal with Tehran.

2022-08-27.title: War Brings Ukraine’s Women New Roles and New Dangers
2022-08-27.abstract: Women have become an all-important force in the fight. But even as they mobilize, they are increasingly bearing the worst of the conflict.

2022-08-27.title: In Finland, a Partying Prime Minister Draws Tuts, and Cheers
2022-08-27.abstract: Leaked videos featuring Sanna Marin dancing have raised the question of whether she is held to a different standard than older, male leaders are.

2022-08-27.title: At One of Canada’s Top Destinations, Tourists Are Back but Much Has Changed
2022-08-27.abstract: The towns in and around Banff National Park are teeming again, but overseas visitors are scarce, as are hotel and restaurant workers.

2022-08-27.title: A Holy British Island, Where the Reckless Try to Outrace the Tide
2022-08-27.abstract: As the cradle of Christianity in northern England, the isle of Lindisfarne attracts 650,000 visitors a year, some of whom ignore the warning signs of when it’s safe to cross a causeway that twice a day is submerged.

2022-08-27.title: The Long Road Ahead for Colonial Reparations
2022-08-27.abstract: Former colonies have felt increasingly empowered to demand compensation from European powers. But even the successful efforts look like exceptions so far.

2022-08-28.title: Pakistan Hit by Deadly Floods of ‘Epic Proportions’
2022-08-28.abstract: More than 1,000 have died since mid-June from flooding that a senior official called a “climate-induced humanitarian disaster.”

2022-08-28.title: U.S. Warships Sail Taiwan Strait, Defying Chinese Pressure
2022-08-28.abstract: Two guided-missile cruisers were the first to visit since China began extensive military drills after Speaker Nancy Pelosi’s visit to Taiwan.

2022-08-28.title: Referendum Seeks to Mend the Open Wound at Australia’s Heart
2022-08-28.abstract: The new prime minister is seeking support for an Aboriginal Voice to Parliament, even enlisting the backing of Shaquille O’Neal. Here’s what it would entail, and why it faces an uncertain path.

2022-08-28.title: Cloud Wars: Mideast Rivalries Rise Along a New Front
2022-08-28.abstract: As climate change makes the region hotter and drier, the U.A.E. is leading the effort to squeeze more rain out of the clouds, and other countries are rushing to keep up.

2022-08-28.title: Bullying and Sex Abuse Scandal Engulfs Elite Danish School
2022-08-28.abstract: Crown Prince Frederik of Denmark and his wife, Princess Mary, pulled their son from the institution, saying that they were “deeply shaken by the reports.”

2022-08-29.title: More Vital Than Ever, Ukraine’s Trains Bind a Land Fractured by War
2022-08-29.abstract: With the skies and ports closed, Ukraine’s railroads have been crucial to efforts to flee, to return, to keep the country going or to simply reclaim some semblance of normal life.

2022-08-29.title: U.N. experts head to the Zaporizhzhia facility on a risky mission after weeks of talks.
2022-08-29.abstract: Both Russia and Ukraine welcomed the news, even as they repeated accusations that the other side was responsible for shelling the area around the plant.

2022-08-29.title: Deep-Sea Riches: Mining a Remote Ecosystem
2022-08-29.abstract: Miles below the surface, harvesting metallic nodules on the ocean floor may speed the green energy revolution but threaten animals found nowhere else on the planet.

2022-08-29.title: In Algeria, Macron Seeks to Reshape a Traumatic Relationship With France
2022-08-29.abstract: On a three-day visit, the French president said he was seeking “truth and acknowledgment” over the war and colonialism.

2022-08-29.title: Secret Data, Tiny Islands and a Quest for Treasure on the Ocean Floor
2022-08-29.abstract: Mining in parts of the Pacific Ocean was meant to benefit poor countries, but an international agency gave a Canadian company access to prized seabed sites with metals crucial to the green energy revolution.

2022-08-30.title: Satellite Images Show Damage From Deadly Flooding in Pakistan
2022-08-30.abstract: Floodwaters have swept buildings off their foundations, washed away bridges and roads and inundated the country’s farmland.

2022-08-30.title: The first shipment of Iranian military drones arrives in Russia.
2022-08-30.abstract: Russia could deploy the drones in its war against Ukraine to conduct air-to-surface attacks, carry out electronic warfare and identify targets, according to U.S. officials.

2022-08-30.title: Iraqi Shiite Factions Turn Their Guns on Each Other in a Deadly New Phase
2022-08-30.abstract: With no functioning government and no common ground, tensions among Shiite political factions have become a deadly matter in Baghdad this week.

2022-08-30.title: ‘Not My Choice.’ A TV Anchor Is Ousted, and Viewers Ask: Was Sexism to Blame?
2022-08-30.abstract: The chief executive of a Canadian news network pushed back against allegations that sex, age or gray hair were factors in the abrupt dismissal of the veteran journalist, Lisa LaFlamme.

2022-08-30.title: How German Companies Are Staring Down Putin’s Threat to Cut Off Gas
2022-08-30.abstract: Industrial producers across Germany are coming up with unique ways to cut their dependence on natural gas as Russia’s war in Ukraine keeps prices high.

2022-08-31.title: How Reagan and Bush overcame skepticism to collaborate with Gorbachev.
2022-08-31.abstract: Mr. Gorbachev was charming and presented himself as a reformer, but neither Ronald Reagan nor George Bush was convinced he was for real. They would both be proved wrong.

2022-08-31.title: How Gorbachev’s ‘perestroika’ vanished.
2022-08-31.abstract: His vision of a “common European home” stretching from Vladivostok to Lisbon has been replaced by a Russia increasingly isolated from the rest of Europe.

2022-08-31.title: The experts’ convoy of armored S.U.V.s traveled toward an active battlefield.
2022-08-31.abstract: KYIV, Ukraine — Experts from the United Nations’ nuclear watchdog agency are expected to visit the imperiled Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant on Thursday, a day after they embarked on one of the most complicated missions in the agency’s history.

2022-08-31.title: U.N. Experts to Inspect Nuclear Power Plant in Ukraine
2022-08-31.abstract: A group of experts with the International Atomic Energy Agency, the United Nations nuclear watchdog, departed from Kyiv, Ukraine, headed to the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant. The team intends to establish a permanent monitoring mission at the Russian-occupied nuclear facility.

2022-08-31.title: ‘Absolute Warfare’: Cartels Terrorize Mexico as Security Forces Fall Short
2022-08-31.abstract: The president disbanded the Federal Police and created the National Guard to tackle soaring violence, but three years later, criminal cartels have expanded their reach.
